text: As I stand at the threshold and enter this space, I feel an odd blend of anticipation and uncertainty. The glass and clean modern lines suggest boundaries—between outside and inside, between observer and participant. Once inside, I am met with the bold "R+D"—a promise of research and development, of minds shaping the future. Yet the blur of movement, the haze of lights, the impersonal displays together evoke a subtle disorientation. I am here to witness innovation, but I feel simultaneously adrift, unmoored, as if the cutting edge carries with it an existential questioning: What role do I play amid these networks and technologies? My presence is both acknowledged and abstracted, echoed in the sterile glow—eager to learn, but quietly aware of a lingering detachment.
